items.title: Avoid any
items.avoid: Using any to bypass the checker.
items.why: Preserves the safety TypeScript exists to give.
items.example: Configure `tsconfig.json` with `"noImplicitAny": true` and `"strict": true` to force this discipline across the project.

items.title: Validate runtime data
items.avoid: Trusting API/model JSON because it's typed.
items.prefer: Validate shapes at runtime (e.g. zod) then use the type.
items.why: Types are erased and can't guard runtime inputs.

items.title: Model fixed values as unions
items.example:
```ts
type LogLevel = 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'
function log(level: LogLevel, msg: string) { ... }
log('warning', '...')   // ← compile error, suggests 'warn'
```

items.title: Pick interface vs type deliberately
items.prefer: interface for object shapes, type for unions/intersections.
items.example: `interface User { name: string }` for shapes. `type Result<T> = T | { error: string }` for unions. Stick to the convention so readers can predict what's where.
